findings.rule_name: Public to sensitive data attack path
findings.title: Public entrypoint aws_lb.admin reaches sensitive asset aws_db_instance.customer
findings.description: ChangeGate detected a high-signal infrastructure attack path.

evidence.message: attack path attack-path-ebb91706401c9280 produced block decision
target group routes to ECS service
resource can send traffic through security group

remediation.summary: Remove the public route to the workload or restrict ingress to approved CIDRs.
steps: Allow sensitive assets only from reviewed workload security groups and roles.
Remove direct routing from public workloads to sensitive datastores or secrets.
Remove the public route to the workload or restrict ingress to approved CIDRs.
Restrict the public entrypoint to approved CIDRs or authenticated edge controls.
Segment the workload from sensitive data stores and secrets.
remediation.why_this_works: Removing any required step breaks the attack path before deployment.

patches.title: Attack path requires topology review
patches.rationale: ChangeGate does not auto-patch multi-resource attack paths because the correct fix depends on service ownership, routing intent, and approved access patterns.

next_steps: Attach evidence of the selected mitigation before apply.
Treat as release-blocking unless a reviewer approves a time-bounded waiver.

findings.rule_name: Internet-facing ALB routes to admin service
findings.title: Internet-facing ALB routes to admin service
findings.description: Detects public load balancer paths to resources that appear to expose admin surfaces.

evidence.message: aws_ecs_service.admin: security group applies to resource (security_groups)
aws_security_group.public: security group allows public ingress (ingress)

remediation.summary: Remove public routing to the admin service or require private/authenticated access.
steps: Confirm downstream services are not tagged as admin or production unless the exposure is intentional.
If public access is required, restrict listener security groups to VPN, zero-trust proxy, or allowlisted CIDRs.
Set the ALB `internal` argument to `true` for private admin services.
remediation.why_this_works: Removing direct public routing to admin workloads prevents unauthenticated internet clients from reaching privileged control surfaces.

patches.title: Prefer internal ALB for admin services
patches.rationale: Snippet is advisory because variable names, module boundaries, and environment intent must be reviewed before use.

next_steps: Attach evidence of the selected mitigation before apply.
Treat as release-blocking unless a reviewer approves a time-bounded waiver.

findings.rule_name: Sensitive Port Is Exposed To Entire Network
findings.title: Sensitive Port Is Exposed To Entire Network
findings.description: A sensitive port, such as port 23 or port 110, is open for the whole network in either TCP or UDP protocol

remediation.summary: Constrain public exposure to the smallest reviewed entry point.
steps: Document any intentional public exposure in policy or a time-bounded waiver.
Prefer private subnets, internal load balancers, or authenticated edge controls.
Remove public CIDRs unless internet access is required.
remediation.why_this_works: Reducing public reachability lowers exploitability and leaves fewer assets directly reachable from the internet.

findings.title: S3 Data should be versioned
findings.description:
Versioning in Amazon S3 is a means of keeping multiple variants of an object in the same bucket.

You can use the S3 Versioning feature to preserve, retrieve, and restore every version of every object stored in your buckets.

With versioning you can recover more easily from both unintended user actions and application failures.

When you enable versioning, also keep in mind the potential costs of storing noncurrent versions of objects. To help manage those costs, consider setting up an S3 Lifecycle configuration.

remediation.summary: Enable versioning to protect against accidental/malicious removal or modification
steps: Add a targeted fix or a time-bounded waiver if the risk is accepted.

findings.title: S3 encryption should use Customer Managed Keys
findings.description:
Encryption using AWS keys provides protection for your S3 buckets. To gain greater control over encryption, such as key rotation, access policies, and auditability, use customer managed keys (CMKs) with SSE-KMS.
Note that SSE-KMS is not supported for S3 server access logging destination buckets; in such cases, use SSE-S3 instead.

remediation.summary: Use SSE-KMS with a customer managed key (CMK)
steps: Add a targeted fix or a time-bounded waiver if the risk is accepted.

resources. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to
remote server administration ports, such as SSH to port 22 and RDP to port 3389.

findings.title: S3 buckets should each define an aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block
findings.description: The "block public access" settings in S3 override individual policies that apply to a given bucket, meaning that all public access can be controlled in one central types for that bucket. It is therefore good practice to define these settings for each bucket in order to clearly define the public access that can be allowed for it.

remediation.summary: Define a aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block for the given bucket to control public access policies
